verses.text: John, in prison, sends to ask, 'Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?' A faith tested. Even the great forerunner has his hour of doubt in the dark.
John, in prison, sends to ask, 'Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?' A faith tested. Even the great forerunner has his hour of doubt in the dark.
John, in prison, sends to ask, 'Art thou he that should come, or do we look for another?' A faith tested. Even the great forerunner has his hour of doubt in the dark.
Jesus answers, Tell John 'the blind receive their sight,' the lame walk, the poor hear the gospel; blessed is he not offended in me. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove He is the Christ.
Jesus answers, Tell John 'the blind receive their sight,' the lame walk, the poor hear the gospel; blessed is he not offended in me. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove He is the Christ.
Jesus answers, Tell John 'the blind receive their sight,' the lame walk, the poor hear the gospel; blessed is he not offended in me. Works speak. The deeds of mercy prove He is the Christ.
Of John He asks, what went ye out to see? a reed shaken with the wind? a prophet? 'yea... and more than a prophet.' True greatness. The plain man of the desert was God's chosen herald.
Of John He asks, what went ye out to see? a reed shaken with the wind? a prophet? 'yea... and more than a prophet.' True greatness. The plain man of the desert was God's chosen herald.
Of John He asks, what went ye out to see? a reed shaken with the wind? a prophet? 'yea... and more than a prophet.' True greatness. The plain man of the desert was God's chosen herald.

John is the promised messenger; none born of women is greater, yet the least in the kingdom is greater than he. Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest office before it.

John is the promised messenger; none born of women is greater, yet the least in the kingdom is greater than he. Privilege of grace. To be in Christ's kingdom is higher than the highest office before it.
From John's day the kingdom 'suffereth violence,' and the violent take it; he is the Elias to come. Earnest seekers. The kingdom is laid hold of by those who press in with all their heart.

This generation is like sulky children, rejecting both John's strictness and the Son of man's freedom; 'wisdom is justified of her children.' Never pleased. The fault-finder will object to anything God sends.

He upbraids the cities that saw His works yet repented not; Tyre and Sidon would have turned in sackcloth. Light refused. Greater privilege brings greater guilt when it is despised.
He upbraids the cities that saw His works yet repented not; Tyre and Sidon would have turned in sackcloth. Light refused. Greater privilege brings greater guilt when it is despised.
He upbraids the cities that saw His works yet repented not; Tyre and Sidon would have turned in sackcloth. Light refused. Greater privilege brings greater guilt when it is despised.
Capernaum, lifted to heaven, shall be brought to hell; more tolerable for Sodom in the day of judgment. Heavy doom. To reject Christ Himself is worse than the sin of Sodom.
Capernaum, lifted to heaven, shall be brought to hell; more tolerable for Sodom in the day of judgment. Heavy doom. To reject Christ Himself is worse than the sin of Sodom.
He thanks the Father for hiding these things from the wise and revealing them to babes; all are delivered to the Son. Humble revelation. God opens His truth to the lowly, not the proud.

'Come unto me, all ye that labour and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest'; take My yoke, for it is easy. Sweet invitation. The weary find rest in coming to Christ.
